column5: 1.2 What is the website ultimately meant to do in the life of your practice? (Long Text) (e.g., articulate a new category, attract the right people, establish authority, express a philosophy, create resonance, filter for alignment)

column4: I am wanting to build people. Above anything I am wanting to build people. My ambition is deep, not wide. So i may only take on 10 students at a time. But I want to go deep with them and not wide. I see the piano as a tool. Like a hammer. But instead of building a house, you are archetecting what the soul looks like with, but with sound. This is my philosophy. So I want to train people and give them the skill to do this. I want to help them with their goals. When someone wants to learn an instrument there is alwyas a deep meaning behind it deep down because deep down, music and instruments are inherintly spiritual.
column5: attracct the right people. Express a philosophy. This is about the art. I provide the service but this is about capturing the art behind it. And the depth. Music and piano is sacred to me, and i want to be able to give that gift to other people of learning to play the piano.
column6: I see piano mentorship as i am giving someone a tool for them to be able to express what is in their soul. It is like an extension of themselves. I see the piano as a body in the sense that the body is here for a big reason to express what is in our soul. Our soul is happy, we smile, our soul is sad, we cry. The piano is the same. But its not through a smile, it is through an entirely different system of expression. Like a major chord or something. Music can reach the extremes of every single emotion and human expression and it trancends words.
column7: I dont want it to be a transactional service. I dont want it to be a shallow money making business. I dont want it to be scalable in the sense of going wide. maybe in going deep. I want to protect the sacredness of music and passing that on. I want to work whole heartedly with each student I have to help them reach their goals.
column8: I am creating a category of its own. I have not been taught how to teach piano. So i am pioneering a new way. That isnt about scaling or the business. I don't need to teach to survive, so I am free to do it out of depth and not desperation. I am not forced to do it, i do it from the fuel of passion.
column9: For the website to express that successfully would mean that all visuals, all copy, all pictures, all sounds, all animations express that. Each little detail needs to reflect that. The website itself needs to be alive with this personality. The deep and not wide. The not desperate. But just putting your truth out there and polarizing the ones who come with you into the vision. Who are captivated by the vision.
column10: some truths that feel difficult are the truths that piano has a very steep learning curve. And being honest there will be times it wont feel fun to practice. But we work on specificalyl discerning for you what makes it worth it. I think also the truth that piano is more than music,
column11: I want them to understand my specifc philosophy on teaching and on music and on instruments and the piano. I want them to know my exclusivity, and my prices, and my style of teaching, and my process.
column12: That every single person has the potential to become great. It doesnt matter who you are, every single person has the potenital to become great. The ones who dont stick with it dont have a strong "WHY". So i spend a lot of time not just on the skills of the piano but on the deeper side with each indivudal. and each person is unique and i want to hear their stories of why they want to learn piano. What thought or vision brings them life when they think of themselves playing piano. Who do they imagine themselves playing in front of? in what context?
column13: I think people will think it will be easy and linear. It is not easy. I think people think that learning the piano will be super fun. A lot of it is not fun. a lot of it is very robotic and repetitive. But it is all learning how to which is good.
column14: I believe they dont focus on the deeper reasons. I think they dont focus on the individual. They focus on reading music, and reading sheet music. But that is the expression of what is in someone elses heart. Not their own. It can feel relatable. but even when they are playing someone elses sheet music it needs to be authentic to their own identity and to archetict what is in their own soul.
column15: It is because it is relational and it is organic and i am with them in a deeper way than just on their piano lesson. i cater the lessons specifically for them and create questions to help them specifically flesh out their vision and their why. And to give them their own uunique voice through the piano.
column16: because it is not just information transfer. But it is shaping the person who is learning aswell.
column17: We understand that failure is inevitagble and sometimes the only way up is down. Like a hike, the path up is not linear. And the formation is in the frustration but the perserverance of that frustration. It is not in the destination. We stare at the destination and we perservere until we get tehre,
column18: My role of exxpression is that the piano is a tool for expression. and each person playing the piano will be unique in crafting "their sound" because the person is unique. I see it as language and words. In the sense Jesus said that out of the heart the mout hspeaks. so a word is only a reflection of the heart. I see that the same. ideally, out of the heart, we playu the piano and what we play is simply a reflection of what is in our heart.
column19: to understand and grip their vision. This is their north star and it needs to happen 1st because it will be what anchors them. It is their light at the end of the tunnel.
column20: It means that we understand that there will be friction. But we also understand that with practice, and even bioloigically how the brain works, it will come a point where it feels frictionless. and second nature where you just play and you dont need to think twice about it, but you just play.
column21: The philosphy of music. That it is so intriciate because it reflects the human soul. and the human soul is so intriciate. but it is so beautiful because in all of it's intricacies, somehow it is still relatable.
column22: we go deep. It is mentorship. It is not shallow. It helps the individual. They aren't put into a system. They are put with a teacher who is organic and relational. It is discovery, not mission. And it is relational, not a system,.
column23: that i put in a lot of work outside of the alloted lesson time. To make it custom and be spoke to each student. And that i dont teach the traditional route of just following the royal conservatory book.  I make my own pathway unique for each student. And if that means the royal conservatory then that is great. But it is about adpating to the students learning style,
column24: It is relational and gives direct specific feedback, not from a system or a program but from a person with experience. They are walking through a maze in first person. but i have walked through that maze and can offer guidance from a birds eye view. Guiding them,

column28: Maybe people who want to write music. or in worship to God. Or people who want to express themselves and learn. Or people who are deep and philisophical maybe. Or a parent who really cares for their kid. A kid who gets the deeper things in life.

column31: Because my brand is unique and my teaching style and playing style is ultimately super unique.
column32: they arent plugging into a corporate system. It is not a day care. But it is for the depth of the art of piano and learning it.
column33: when you own the land, you can build whatever you want on it. This meaninng that when you own all the technique and master it in piano. All the theory and scales, you can accurately express what you want to. Even intricate stuff like the sound of childhood for example. For me music means so much. it is a way to tell my own story through writing songs, to help people. and helping other people scribe their songs and capture the depth of their songs, through producing for them.,
column34:
Your relationship to sacredness and sound
our own philosophy of patience and listening
 Your experience accompanying people through emotionally significant moments, and intricate moments, like weddings. column35: That piano is much deeper than people think it is. That it is more of a life philosophy than anything. in the same way that chess players look at it as a metaphor for life. piano is the same.
